prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `470c91de`.
codex_response:
Each non-background rectangle is missing exactly one corner, with that corner replaced by `8`. The verifier in [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_470c91de/verifier.py#L4) restores the missing corner and shifts the completed rectangle one cell diagonally outward in the direction of that marked corner.

I used the Opus hint and discarded the Sonnet hint. Sonnet’s “global reorganization / better separation” description was wrong; the actual rule is purely local per rectangle, documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_470c91de/notes.md). The generator in [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_470c91de/generator.py#L61) matches the family by sampling `10x10` and `11x11` `7`-background grids with `2-4` distinct rectangles, replacing one corner per rectangle with `8`, biasing placements toward edge-touching layouts, and enforcing non-overlap in both input and shifted-output space. No shared primitive was added.
